===''Pokémon the Series''=== [[File:Ignitionshipping.png|thumb|250px|Flint and Volkner]] ====''[[Pokémon the Series: Diamond and Pearl]]''==== Flint was first mentioned in ''[[DP035|An Elite Meet and Greet!]]'' by [[Lucian]], and again in ''[[DP099|A Trainer and Child Reunion!]]'' by [[Officer Jenny]]. Flint made his physical debut in ''[[DP165|Flint Sparks the Fire!]]''. He noticed {{Ash}}'s determination as he walked by the [[Sunyshore Gym]], and went on to escort the group directly to [[Volkner]], who was hiding away in the [[Sunyshore Tower]]. After Volkner declined Ash's challenge to a [[Gym]] {{pkmn|battle}}, Flint took {{ashfr|the group}} to a café run by his old friend, the [[Proprietor]]. Flint explained that he used to battle the Sunyshore street hoodlums with his {{TP|Flint|Chimchar}} when he was younger. He came across Volkner one day and defeated him in battle, though the next time the pair met, Volkner won. The two soon became rivals and later good friends after having a Tag Battle against the Proprietor. After turning 20, Flint left his [[hometown]] and eventually became a member of the [[Sinnoh]] [[Elite Four]]. At the same time, Volkner became the Sunyshore Gym Leader. [[File:Young Flint.png|left|thumb|250px|Young Flint and his Chimchar]] However, Flint constantly returned to Sunyshore City to convince the increasingly despondent Volkner to start battling again. After Volkner refused Ash's challenge for a battle again, Flint battled Ash in his place and defeated [[Ash's Pikachu]], {{AP|Infernape}}, and {{AP|Buizel}} with his {{p|Infernape}} easily. In the process, Volkner observed the match and became reinvigorated upon observing Ash's teamwork with his Pokémon. Flint was glad to see this development and watched as Volkner reconsidered his decision to turn Ash away. In ''[[DP166|The Fleeing Tower of Sunyshore!]]'', Flint sat in the grandstands to watch Volkner go up against Ash. The Gym battle was interrupted by {{TRT}} after they stole the Sunyshore Tower and caused the city to have a power outage. Flint, Volkner, and Ash all jumped onto the tower as Team Rocket was beginning to rocket into the sky, though Flint lost his grip and feel into the ocean below. However, he worked with {{an|Brock}} to provide pedal power to the local [[Pokémon Center]] during the crisis. Flint later watched as Volkner landed the Sunyshore Tower back onto the ground, and he wished {{an|Dawn}} the best with her upcoming {{pkmn|Contest}}s as she and her friends left Sunyshore. In ''[[DP179|The Eighth Wonder of the Sinnoh World!]]'', Flint watched Volkner battle Ash again. In the [[DP180|next episode]], he saved {{si|Kenny}}'s {{p|Floatzel}} from Team Rocket and won against [[Jasmine]] in an {{wp|exhibition game|exhibition match}}. He also revealed that he would be challenging {{an|Cynthia}} for the title of [[Sinnoh League]] {{pkmn|Champion}}. Ash and his friends watched Flint battle Cynthia at the {{DL|Pokémon League|Champion League}} on TV in ''[[DP191|Memories are Made of Bliss!]]''. As mentioned by the emcee, Flint's Infernape managed to defeat two of Cynthia's Pokémon. However, Flint lost the match after his Infernape was defeated by [[Cynthia's Garchomp]]. Flint made a cameo appearance in the ending credits of ''[[M13|Zoroark: Master of Illusions]]''. ====''[[Pokémon Journeys: The Series]]''==== [[File:Flint anime.png|thumb|250px|Flint in {{aniseries|JN}}]] In ''[[JN100|Meeting Up with the Monarch!]]'', Flint had a [[World Coronation Series]] battle against {{an|Leon}}, where he used Infernape against [[Leon's Charizard]], only to lose, causing his rank to drop from 7 to 8. In ''[[JN109|Aim For The Eight!]]'', Flint was revealed to have fallen out of the Master Class. In ''[[JN117|The Fiery Road to Mastership!]]'', Flint was shown watching Cynthia's [[Masters Eight Tournament]] match against {{an|Iris}}. ====Character==== Flint is a supportive friend, and despite all of his success as a member of the [[Elite Four]], he made several efforts to reach out to [[Volkner]] over the years. He is also passionate about battling and a prolific user of defense, a strategy he used to defeat {{Ash}} and later [[Jasmine]] of the [[Olivine Gym]]. In addition to strategy, in ''[[DP180|Four Roads Diverged in a Pokémon Port!]]'', Flint told Ash that having confidence in oneself is just as critical to being a successful [[Pokémon Trainer]]. ====Pokémon==== This listing is of Flint's known {{OBP|Pokémon|species}} in {{aniseries|PTS}}: {| width="100%" |- | {{ActivePoké|Flint|Infernape|Flint Infernape.png|fire|fighting|size=200px|fir=Chimchar|mid=Monferno}} | |} Flint also has at least five more Pokémon, which he used during his battle against Cynthia. ====Achievements==== =====Badges obtained===== This listing is of the [[Badge]]s Flint has obtained in the [[Sinnoh]] [[region]]: * At least eight Badges (prior to ''[[DP035|An Elite Meet and Greet!]]'') [[File:Flint Ranking WCS.png|250px|thumb|Flint's World Coronation Series ranking (prior to ''[[JN107|Big Brother to the Rescue!]]'')]] =====Pokémon League ranking===== Flint has competed in the following [[Pokémon League Conference]]s: * [[Lily of the Valley Conference]] - Winner (prior to ''[[DP035|An Elite Meet and Greet!]]'') =====World Coronation Series===== Flint has competed in the World Coronation Series. His rank and class are as follows: * Rank 7 (Master Class; prior to ''[[JN100|Meeting Up with the Monarch!]]'') * Rank 8 (Master Class; ''[[JN100|Meeting Up with the Monarch!]]'') * Rank unknown (Ultra Class; prior to ''[[JN107|Big Brother to the Rescue!]]'') ====Voice actors==== {{vatable|color={{fire color}}|bordercolor={{fire color light}} |de=Patrick Roche |ja=奈良徹 ''[[Toru Nara]]'' |en=[[Tom Wayland]] ([[DP165]]-[[DP180]])
